tasks.subject: Task 1: Extract `_build_dump` helper + 3 unit tests
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Move the inline dump-construction body from `tests/conftest.py:359-382` into a new pure helper `tests/_thread_dump_helper.py::_build_dump(threads, exitstatus) -> str`, refactor `pytest_sessionfinish` to call it (behaviour-preserving), and cover the helper with 3 unit tests. Existing `non_daemon_extras` fast-path filter preserved.

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/_thread_dump_helper.py`
- Modify: `tests/conftest.py` lines ~338-389
- Test: `tests/test_post_session_dump.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `tests/_thread_dump_helper.py` defines `_build_dump(threads, exitstatus) -> str` with typed signature + Google docstring
- [ ] `pytest_sessionfinish` imports and calls `_build_dump`; inline `io.StringIO()` body deleted
- [ ] `non_daemon_extras` fast-path filter survives intact
- [ ] All 3 unit tests pass under `pixi run pytest tests/test_post_session_dump.py -v`
- [ ] `pixi run ruff check` zero new findings on touched files
- [ ] `pixi run mypy` zero new findings on touched files

**Verify:** `pixi run pytest tests/test_post_session_dump.py -v` → 3 passed in <2 s.

**Steps:** see plan §Task 1 for full step-by-step (8 steps: write 3 failing tests → confirm fail → create helper module → confirm pass → refactor conftest → re-confirm pass → lint/typecheck → commit).

Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-pytest-thread-leak-diagnostic.md`
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-pytest-thread-leak-diagnostic-design.md`


tasks.subject: Task 2: Add pytest_configure faulthandler timer + e2e smoke
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Layer the C-side `faulthandler.dump_traceback_later(15, repeat=False, file=sys.stderr)` timer on `pytest_configure` so a pytest hang that never reaches `pytest_sessionfinish` (collection / fixture stall) still produces a stack dump within 15 s. Cancel the timer at the top of `pytest_sessionfinish` so healthy runs do NOT also dump from the timer. Add one end-to-end subprocess test that asserts the dump fires on a leaked non-daemon thread.

**Files:**
- Modify: `tests/conftest.py` — add `pytest_configure(config)` above existing `pytest_sessionfinish`; prepend `faulthandler.cancel_dump_traceback_later()` inside sessionfinish
- Test: `tests/test_post_session_dump_e2e.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `pytest_configure(config)` calls `faulthandler.enable()` then `faulthandler.dump_traceback_later(15, repeat=False, file=sys.stderr, exit=False)`
- [ ] First executable line of `pytest_sessionfinish` (post-docstring + import) is `faulthandler.cancel_dump_traceback_later()`
- [ ] `tests/test_post_session_dump_e2e.py` spawns pytest as subprocess (`timeout=30 s`) on a temp leaky-thread module; asserts stderr contains `=== POST-SESSION THREAD DUMP ===` AND `daemon=False` AND `e2e_leaker`
- [ ] `pixi run pytest tests/test_post_session_dump_e2e.py -v` passes in <20 s
- [ ] Healthy `pixi run pytest tests/test_pool.py -v 2>&1 | rg "POST-SESSION|Timeout \\(0:00:15\\)"` emits ONE `clean (...)` line and NO `Timeout (0:00:15)!` line
- [ ] `pixi run ruff check` + `pixi run mypy` zero new findings

**Verify:** `pixi run pytest tests/test_post_session_dump_e2e.py -v` → 1 passed in <20 s.

**Steps:** see plan §Task 2 for full step-by-step (7 steps: write e2e first → confirm pass against existing hook → add pytest_configure → add cancel call → re-run unit+e2e+sanity → lint/typecheck → commit).

Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-pytest-thread-leak-diagnostic.md`
Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-pytest-thread-leak-diagnostic-design.md`
